LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT MCCORMICK & COMPANY, INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO QUASH NOTICE OF DEPOSITION AND FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. MCCORMICK & COMPANY SHALL PRODUCE ITS ENTITY DESIGNEES IN SAN FRANCISCO, THE LOCATION NOTICE BY PLAINTIFF. TOYOTA MOTOR CORP. V. SUPERIOR COURT IS DISTINGUISHABLE FROM THE INSTANT ACTION. THE TOYOTA COURT'S DECISION WAS MADE IN THE CONTEXT OF INDIVIDUALS WHOSE DEPOSITIONS HAD BEEN NOTICED, NOT A CORPORATION NOTICED FOR DEPOSITION PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2025.230. THE TOYOTA COURT EXPLICITLY CAUTIONED, "WE EXPRESS NO OPINION, HOWEVER, AS TO WHETHER OUR ANALYSIS OR THE CONCLUSIONS WE HAVE REACHED IN THIS OPINION WOULD OR SHOULD EXTEND OR APPLY TO A COURT ORDER MADE PURSUANT TO SECTION 2025.230 WHICH PROVIDES FOR THE CIRCUMSTANCE WHERE THE DEPONENT NAMED IS NOT A NATURAL PERSON." (TOYOTA MOTOR CORP. V. SUP.CT. (2011) 197 CAL.APP.4TH 1107, 1125, FN. 20 (INTERNAL QUOTATIONS OMITTED).) THE COURT TOOK THE MONETARY SANCTION ISSUE UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: ERNEST H. Off Calendar Feb-27-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. LAURENCE D. HAVESON OF THE CHANLER GROUP, TEL. NO. (510) 848-8880 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHELLE P. ALBORZSFAR OF THE HOGAN LOVELLS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 374-2300 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court ruled as follows: DEFENDANT MCCORMICK & COMPANY, INCORPORATED's Motion To Quash Notice Of Deposition And For A Protective Order is Denied. McCormick & Company shall produce its entity designees in San Francisco, the location notice by Plaintiff. Toyota Motor Corp. v. Superior Court is distinguishable from the instant action. The Toyota Court's decision was made in the context of individuals whose depositions had been noticed, not a corporation noticed for deposition pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.230. The Toyota Court explicitly cautioned, "we express no opinion, however, as to whether our analysis or the conclusions we have reached in this opinion would or should extend or apply to a court order made pursuant to section 2025.230 which provides for the circumstance where the deponent named is not a natural person." (Toyota Motor Corp. v. Sup.Ct. (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 1107, 1125, fn. 20 (internal quotations omitted).)
The court took the monetary sanction issue under submission. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Not reported. =302/EHG
